story.title: Dr. Patel's Light Puzzles
story.text:
My name is Dr. Aisha Patel. I am a scientist at NASA. I study new planets that are very far away. I cannot see these planets directly. I only see the light from their star.

This light tells me a story from the past. A very distant star sends us very old light. This is the Light Travel Time. The Star Distance is very important. A farther star looks dimmer to me. Some stars are very powerful. They have high Luminosity. This makes them look brighter from Earth. I use my computer to model these things. My model calculates the Apparent Brightness we see.

But my job is more complex. I also look at the star's color. A blue star is hotter than a red star. Dust in space also changes the light. My model must include these details, too. Every star's light is a puzzle. I love solving these puzzles to find new worlds.

margin_defs.definition: a simple version of something complex

check_prompts: Does light from a far star reach us right away?

vocabulary.definition: The distance light travels in one year. It is a unit of distance, not time.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The nearest star system to us is more than four light years away.

vocabulary.definition: The true, actual amount of light and energy a star gives off.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A giant star has a much higher luminosity than a small star.

vocabulary.definition: How bright a star looks to us when we see it from Earth.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A close, dim star can have a greater apparent brightness than a far, luminous star.

vocabulary.definition: A science rule that says light gets much weaker as it spreads out over distance.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Because of the inverse square law, a star that is twice as far away looks four times dimmer.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Patel's basic model includes Star Distance and Star Luminosity. What else does she add to her model to make it better?
discussion_cards.frame: To make her model better, Dr. Patel also adds information about a star's ___ and ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Why is the light Dr. Patel sees 'a story from the past'?
discussion_cards.frame: The light is from the past because it takes a very long time to ___ from the star to Earth.

discussion_cards.prompt: If two stars have the same luminosity, what would make one star have a greater apparent brightness?
discussion_cards.frame: If two stars have the same luminosity, the one that is ___ to Earth would look brighter.

organizer.title: What Makes a Star Look Bright or Dim?
organizer.left_header: Makes a Star Look BRIGHTER From Earth
organizer.right_header: Makes a Star Look DIMMER From Earth

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that stars are ___.
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that scientists use a star's light to ___.
reflection_frames.wonder: Now I wonder how scientists measure a star's ___.
